title: pleopeltis lanceola
intro: Pleopeltis lanceola, also known as the lanceleaf polypody fern, is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and acting as an antioxidant, making it a popular herbal remedy in many cultures. Pleopeltis lanceola is typically prepared as a tea or tincture and can also be used in herbal infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
